parameters.content:
## 👀 Watch Channel for Messages
Listens for every new message in the configured channel. Plain text messages and unsupported file types are filtered out by the guards downstream, so this trigger stays simple and catches everything.

## 🪞 Guard: Ignore Self & No-File Filter
Two checks: ignore the bot's own posts (otherwise it'd react to its own replies in a loop), and silently skip plain text messages with no file attached. Replace `UYOURBOTID` with your bot's Slack user ID – find it under your Slack app's **Bot User** settings.

## 🚫 Reply: Unsupported File Type
Fires only when someone uploads an actual file the Extractor can't read – Word docs, videos, archives, etc. Plain text messages are already filtered out upstream, so recruiters who just chat in the channel don't get spammed with error replies.

## 🛂 Guard: Supported MIME
Only `application/pdf`, `image/png`, and `image/jpeg` pass through. The Extractor doesn't accept Word docs, so DOCX is rejected with a friendly Slack reply asking the recruiter to share a PDF instead.

## ⬇️ Download File from Slack
Slack's file URLs are private – they need a `Bearer xoxb-...` token to download. Uses a **Header Auth** credential (name: `Authorization`, value: `Bearer <your-bot-token>`) to fetch the binary, which is then passed to the Extractor.

## 🧠 Extract & Format
Runs the CV through the **easybits Extractor** to pull 8 structured fields: `full_name`, `location`, `total_years_experience`, `top_skills`, `last_three_roles`, `education`, `salary_expectations`, `linkedin_url`. The formatter then builds two outputs: a clean Slack summary, and a Block Kit card payload carrying the structured data in the Save button's value field.

## 💬 Post Summary to Thread
Posts a markdown-formatted summary (name, location, years, skills, roles, education) as a reply in the thread of the uploaded CV. Recruiters can read it without leaving the channel.

## 🎛️ Post Action Card to Slack
Sends a second message with two interactive buttons: **💾 Save to Sheet** and **Dismiss**. Uses a direct `chat.postMessage` HTTP call because the n8n Slack node currently has a bug that ignores blocks payloads (`invalid_arguments` / fallback text only). Pairs with **Workflow B** which handles the button clicks.

# 👋 CV Slack Bot – Drop a CV, Get an Instant Summary
(powered by easybits)

A recruiter drops a CV file into a Slack channel. Within seconds, the bot replies in-thread with a structured summary – name, location, years of experience, top 3 skills, last 3 roles, education, salary expectations, and LinkedIn – plus an action card to save the candidate to a Google Sheet for follow-up.

Built for fast, mid-call lookups (not batch screening). Pairs with **Workflow B** which handles the Save to Sheet button.

## ⚙️ How It Works
1. **Watch Channel for Messages** listens for new messages in your CV channel
2. **Guard: Ignore Self & No-File Filter** skips the bot's own posts and plain text messages
3. **Guard: Supported MIME** filters to PDF, PNG, or JPG only
4. **Download File from Slack** fetches the binary using a Bearer token
5. **easybits: Extract CV Fields** pulls 8 structured fields from the CV
6. **Build Summary & Action Card** formats the Slack summary + Block Kit payload
7. **Post Summary to Thread** posts the markdown summary
8. **Post Action Card to Slack** posts the buttons via direct API call

## 🛠️ Setup Guide
### 1. Install the easybits Extractor node
**n8n Cloud:**
- Open n8n → **Settings → Community nodes → Install**
- Enter `@easybits/n8n-nodes-extractor` and click install
- Refresh – the **easybits Extractor** node will appear in your node panel

**Self-hosted n8n:**
- SSH into your n8n instance
- Run: `npm install @easybits/n8n-nodes-extractor` (in your n8n root directory)
- Restart your n8n instance
- The Extractor node will appear after restart

**Get your API key:**
- Sign up at `https://go.easybits.tech/cn` (free plan supports up to 10 fields per Extractor — this workflow uses 8)
- Create an Extractor for CVs with the 8 fields documented in the **Extract & Format** sticky
- Copy your API key, then in n8n: **Credentials → Add credential → easybits Extractor API** and paste

### 2. Create the Slack app
Go to api.slack.com/apps → **Create New App**:
- **Bot Token Scopes:** `chat:write`, `files:read`, `channels:history`, `channels:read`
- **Event Subscriptions:** subscribe to `message.channels`
- Install to workspace and copy the **Bot User OAuth Token** (`xoxb-...`)
- Invite the bot to your CV channel: `/invite @YourBotName`

### 3. Set up n8n credentials
- **Slack API** credential – for the trigger node
- **Header Auth** credential – name: `Authorization`, value: `Bearer xoxb-...` (uses your bot token). Needed for the file download and the action card HTTP call.

### 4. Configure the workflow
- Set the channel in **Watch Channel for Messages**
- Replace `UYOURBOTID` in **Guard: Ignore Self & No-File Filter** with your actual bot user ID
- Attach the Slack and Header Auth credentials to all nodes that need them

### 5. Activate
- Toggle workflow Active (top right)
- Copy the trigger's **Production URL**
- Paste into your Slack app's Event Subscriptions → Request URL → Save
- Drop a CV in the channel to test
